name: transaction
description: The money-movement ledger. Answers 'where has every dollar flowed across accounts?'. FUNDAMENTALLY DISTINCT from Membership (the contract), Invoice (money owed), and Payment (a successful capture) — Transaction is the append-only double-entry ledger that records the movement itself. Ships TWO tables: `transactions` (root record — one row per business-level money movement; ties together the source + destination + amount + kind of movement) and `transaction_ledger_entries` (double-entry line items — debit + credit pair for every movement — enforces the balanced-books invariant). Every transaction has AT LEAST TWO ledger entries summing to zero (double-entry principle). Twelve transaction kinds (invoice_payment / refund_issued / chargeback_debit / coupon_credit / tax_remittance / fee_charge / adjustment / write_off / bank_deposit / bank_withdrawal / currency_conversion / opening_balance) map onto twelve chart-of-accounts entries (revenue / accounts_receivable / cash / refunds_owed / refunds_expense / tax_payable / gateway_fees / bank_deposits / write_offs / coupons_expense / chargebacks_expense / accounts_payable / prepayments / deferred_revenue). Transactions IMMUTABLE after commit — reversal happens via a NEW offsetting transaction (pointing at the original via source_transaction_id + reversal_reason), never mutation. Multi-currency support: transaction.currency frozen at commit; if a cross-currency movement (tenant base currency ≠ transaction currency), exchange_rate_to_base + base_amount_cents captured atomically. Every ledger entry constraint: exactly one of debit_cents / credit_cents > 0 per row. Invariant: SUM(debit_cents) == SUM(credit_cents) per transaction (enforced by TransactionObserver at commit + reasserted nightly by LedgerReconciler). Ships the `TransactionRecorder` service used atomically by invoice / payment / refund / chargeback modules during their own operations — every invoice paid → one transaction recorded → [debit: cash, credit: accounts_receivable] ledger entries; every refund issued → one transaction recorded → [debit: refunds_owed, credit: cash] + [debit: refunds_expense, credit: revenue]. Read-only HTTP surface (admin + finance role only) — writes ONLY through internal service. Reversal via POST /transactions/{txn}/reverse (admin-only, requires reason + approval). LedgerImbalanceDetected is the module's CRITICAL P0 event — it should NEVER fire; if it does, ledger is broken + new transactions halt until resolved.
planned_consumers: payment (Wave 4.5 — every successful payment capture records an invoice_payment transaction via TransactionRecorder)
refund (Wave 4.5 — every refund records a refund_issued transaction with offsetting ledger entries)
chargeback (Wave 4.5 — every chargeback records a chargeback_debit transaction with the disputed amount)

tenant.description: Tenant-facing READ-ONLY ledger surface. Admin + finance role only. Every route carries `sanctum` guard + `X-Application-Id` header + `entitlement.enforce:transaction_capture`. Write actions are NEVER exposed — transactions are recorded ONLY through internal service calls by invoice / payment / refund / chargeback modules. Reversal is an admin-only escape hatch that creates a NEW offsetting transaction (never mutates).

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant support tooling — ops + support review ledger health, escalate imbalances, force reconciliation runs during audits.
